sourceMovie.title: In the Valley of Elah
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: In the Valley of Elah (2007) stuck because it commits to its history premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Paul Haggis's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kiss the Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If In the Valley of Elah clicked for you, Kiss the Girls (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after In the Valley of Elah?
faq.answer: Try Kiss the Girls, Edge of Darkness, Reptile—each shares DNA with In the Valley of Elah while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is In the Valley of Elah?
faq.answer: In the Valley of Elah (2007) is best described as History, Drama, Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is In the Valley of Elah worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
